_meta.edition: 2022 (South African national IMCI adaptation; HIV/ART, RUTF, RTHB, social-grant content confirm it is not the WHO 2014 generic booklet)
_meta.extracted_by: scripts/parse_imci_booklet.py (pypdf layout mode) + manual transcription of the classification tables
_meta.status: DRAFT TRANSCRIPTION -- NOT clinician-reviewed. This is the reviewable source for extending src/tools/imci_protocol.py (assess) and src/sft/extended_protocol.py. Every new/changed branch must be signed off by a qualified clinician against the current national adaptation before it drives training data. See adtc-sft-corpus-built memory.

severity_tiers.yellow: specific treatment at clinic + follow-up (middle row)

modeled_status_legend.modeled: already in imci_protocol.assess() with a matching label
modeled_status_legend.new_2022: present in the 2022 booklet but NOT in either engine
modeled_status_legend.divergent: engine models this branch but the 2022 booklet's signs/labels/drugs differ from the engine's 2014 basis
modeled_status_legend.engine_gap: in the published booklet (2014 generic AND 2022) but NOT in either engine -- an engine-coverage gap, NOT a version difference

_meta._correction_2026_07_20: Re-tagged after cross-checking data/imci-chart-booklet.pdf (WHO generic March 2014). Several rows first tagged new_2022/divergent are actually present in the 2014 generic too (SpO2<90% oximetry, amoxicillin first-line, wheeze/salbutamol, persistent diarrhoea, dysentery+ciprofloxacin). Those are engine_gap (subset vs full booklet), not version differences. The two booklets agree far more than assumed.

general_danger_signs.question: Check for general danger signs (all children)
assess_signs: not able to drink or breastfeed

classifications.label: very_severe_disease
classifications.treatment: pre-referral treatment, keep warm, refer urgently

cough_or_difficult_breathing.question: Does the child have a cough or difficult breathing?
assess_signs: oxygen saturation if pulse oximeter available

classifications.label: severe_pneumonia_or_very_severe_disease
classifications.treatment: oxygen; salbutamol if wheeze; nebulised adrenaline+prednisone if stridor; ceftriaxone IM; cotrimoxazole; refer urgently
classifications.divergence: SpO2<90% oximetry trigger is in BOTH 2014 and 2022 (2014 p.'if pulse oximeter available, refer if <90%'); NOT a 2022 addition. Engine simply omits oximetry input.

classifications.label: pneumonia
classifications.treatment: amoxicillin 5 days; assess for TB if cough >14 days; soothe throat; follow-up 2 days
classifications.note_2014_check: amoxicillin first-line is the 2014 generic standard too (not a 2022 change).

classifications.label: cough_or_cold
triggers: no signs of pneumonia or very severe disease
classifications.treatment: soothe throat; follow-up 5 days if not improving; assess TB if cough >14 days

wheeze_subchart.note: Wheeze/salbutamol sub-classification on the cough branch. Present in the 2014 generic booklet too (engine_gap, not 2022-new). assess() models no wheeze at all.

classifications.label: wheeze
triggers: or all other children with wheeze
classifications.treatment: salbutamol via spacer 5 days; salbutamol+prednisone if referring a severe classification; follow-up 5 days if still wheezing
classifications.note_2014_check: wheeze/salbutamol sub-branch is in the 2014 generic booklet; engine models no wheeze. Not 2022-new.

diarrhoea.question: Does the child have diarrhoea?
assess_signs: offer fluid (not able to drink / drinks eagerly)

dehydration_classifications.label: severe_dehydration
triggers: two of: lethargic/unconscious, sunken eyes, not able to drink or drinking poorly, skin pinch goes back very slowly
dehydration_classifications.treatment: Plan C; refer urgently; low-blood-sugar test; ORS sips en route

dehydration_classifications.label: some_dehydration
triggers: two of: restless/irritable, sunken eyes, drinks eagerly/thirsty, skin pinch goes back slowly
dehydration_classifications.treatment: Plan B; zinc 2 weeks; follow-up 2 days

dehydration_classifications.label: no_dehydration
triggers: not enough signs for severe/some dehydration
dehydration_classifications.treatment: Plan A; zinc 2 weeks; follow-up 5 days if not improving

persistent_diarrhoea_subchart.note: 'AND IF DIARRHOEA 14 DAYS OR MORE' -- a SEPARATE simultaneous classification present in BOTH the 2014 generic and 2022 booklets. imci_protocol.py does NOT model persistent diarrhoea (engine_gap).

classifications.label: severe_persistent_diarrhoea
triggers: AND dehydration present or losing weight
classifications.treatment: treat dehydration; refer urgently; additional Vitamin A dose

classifications.label: persistent_diarrhoea
classifications.treatment: counsel on feeding; Vitamin A; zinc 2 weeks; follow-up 5 days

dysentery_subchart.note: 'AND IF BLOOD IN STOOL' -- separate classification. Not modeled by engine.

classifications.label: severe_dysentery
classifications.treatment: refer urgently; keep warm; low-blood-glucose test

classifications.label: dysentery
classifications.treatment: ciprofloxacin 3 days; follow-up 2 days
classifications.divergence: ciprofloxacin is the 2014 generic drug too (NOT nalidixic acid -- that was the older 2005 booklet). Corrected.

fever.question: Does the child have fever? (history, feel, or axillary temp >=37.5C)
assess_signs: decide malaria risk (lives in / visited malaria zone in past 4 weeks)
rapid malaria test if malaria risk
cold with runny nose / other adequate cause of fever

classifications.label: suspected_meningitis
classifications.treatment: ceftriaxone IM; paracetamol if temp>=38; refer urgently
classifications.divergence: genuine 2022 difference: 2014 label is VERY SEVERE FEBRILE DISEASE; 2022 renames to SUSPECTED MENINGITIS and adds bulging fontanelle. Underlying trigger (danger sign / stiff neck) unchanged.

classifications.label: malaria
triggers: malaria risk AND malaria test positive
classifications.treatment: treat for malaria if positive & child >12 months; paracetamol; notify confirmed cases; follow-up 2 days
classifications.divergence: extended_protocol has 'malaria' / 'fever_malaria_test_required'; 2022 adds the malaria-test-not-available age<12m urgent-referral rule

classifications.label: fever_no_malaria
triggers: OR malaria test not done and a cold/other adequate cause found
classifications.treatment: treat other causes; paracetamol; assess TB if fever >7 days; follow-up 2 days

classifications.label: fever_unspecified_malaria_not_assessed
triggers: no malaria risk assessment performed (engine's simplified branch)
classifications.treatment: paracetamol; consider TB if fever >7 days; follow-up 2 days
classifications.note: engine's simplified stand-in for the full malaria branch

measles.question: Consider measles if fever + generalised rash + (runny nose OR cough OR red eyes)

classifications.label: severe_complicated_measles
classifications.treatment: Vitamin A; chloramphenicol eye ointment if cornea clouding/eye pus; amoxicillin; refer urgently; immunise close contacts

classifications.label: measles_with_eye_or_mouth_complications
classifications.treatment: Vitamin A; chloramphenicol eye ointment 7d; chlorhexidine for ulcers; follow-up 2 days

classifications.label: measles
triggers: measles now or in last 3 months, no complications
classifications.treatment: Vitamin A; isolate 5 days; immunise close contacts; follow-up if needed
classifications.note: 2022 folds test-confirmed / test-pending measles into treatment notes; extended_protocol label is 'measles'

ear_problem.question: Does the child have an ear problem?

classifications.label: mastoiditis
classifications.treatment: ceftriaxone IM; paracetamol; refer urgently

classifications.label: acute_ear_infection
triggers: OR ear pain that wakes the child at night
classifications.treatment: amoxicillin 5 days; dry wicking if discharge; paracetamol; follow-up 5 days

classifications.label: chronic_ear_infection
triggers: pus draining for 14 days or more
classifications.treatment: dry wicking; ear drops if available; follow-up 14 days

classifications.label: no_ear_infection
triggers: no ear pain (or pain that does not wake child) AND no pus
classifications.treatment: no additional treatment

sore_throat_3y_plus.question: Does the child (3 years or older) have a sore throat?

classifications.label: streptococcal_sore_throat
triggers: one or more of: enlarged tonsils, white/yellow exudate, scarlatiniform rash
AND no runny nose AND no cough
classifications.treatment: penicillin; treat pain/fever; soothe throat; follow-up 5 days
classifications.note_2014_check: confirmed absent from the 2014 generic booklet -- genuine 2022 SA-adaptation addition.

classifications.label: sore_throat_non_streptococcal
triggers: not enough signs for streptococcal sore throat
classifications.treatment: soothe throat with a safe remedy

malnutrition.question: Check all children for acute malnutrition / nutritional status

classifications.label: complicated_severe_acute_malnutrition
triggers: oedema of both feet OR WFL/H z-score < -3 OR MUAC <11.5cm
AND one of: any danger sign / other red or yellow classification / weighs <=4kg / <6 months / fails appetite test
classifications.treatment: low-blood-sugar test; keep warm; antibiotic; F75 stabilising feed; Vitamin A; refer urgently

classifications.label: uncomplicated_severe_acute_malnutrition
classifications.treatment: amoxicillin 5d; Vitamin A; RUTF; counsel feeding; assess HIV/TB; follow-up 7 days

classifications.label: moderate_acute_malnutrition
triggers: WFL/H between -3 and -2 z-score OR MUAC 11.5-12.5cm
classifications.treatment: Vitamin A + deworming; RUTF/supplements; assess HIV/TB; follow-up 7 days

classifications.label: growth_problem
triggers: losing weight OR unsatisfactory weight gain OR low/very low weight OR low length-for-age (<24 months)
classifications.treatment: assess feeding & counsel; deworm + Vitamin A if due; social-grant referral; follow-up 7-14 days
classifications.note: the growth-curve/RTHB nutritional-status tier is a 2022 South-African-adaptation addition

classifications.label: overweight_obese
classifications.treatment: dietary counselling; healthy eating; physical activity

classifications.label: no_acute_malnutrition
triggers: weight normal AND satisfactory weight gain AND WFL/H >= -2 z-score OR MUAC >=12.5cm
classifications.treatment: praise caregiver; assess feeding if <2 years

classifications.label: severe_anaemia
triggers: severe palmar pallor OR Hb <7 g/dl
classifications.treatment: refer urgently

classifications.label: anaemia
triggers: some palmar pallor OR Hb 7 up to 11 g/dl
classifications.triggers_note: do NOT give iron if child is receiving RUTF
classifications.treatment: iron + iron-rich foods; deworm if due; follow-up 14 days

classifications.label: no_anaemia
classifications.treatment: assess feeding & counsel if <2 years

hiv.question: Check all children for HIV infection (child test + mother status + features of HIV)
hiv.note: Complex multi-input branch: child HIV test result, ARV prophylaxis status, mother's HIV status, and a count of clinical features of HIV. Full transcription of every outcome row deferred -- captured at the label level below. 2014 generic has a coarser HIV branch (CONFIRMED / HIV EXPOSED); the finer symptomatic/possible/unlikely tiers are 2022-SA.

classifications.label: confirmed_hiv_infection
triggers: positive HIV test in child OR child on ART
classifications.treatment: six steps for ART initiation; cotrimoxazole from 6 weeks; long-term follow-up

classifications.label: hiv_exposed
triggers: infant receiving ARV prophylaxis OR negative HIV test while still breastfeeding / <6 weeks since breastfeeding
classifications.treatment: complete ARV prophylaxis; repeat HIV PCR per schedule; follow-up

classifications.label: suspected_symptomatic_hiv
triggers: 3 or more features of HIV infection
classifications.treatment: counsel & offer HIV testing; reclassify on result; long-term follow-up
classifications.note_2014_check: 2014 generic HIV branch is coarser (CONFIRMED / HIV EXPOSED only); this finer tier is 2022-SA.

classifications.label: possible_hiv_infection
triggers: 1-2 features of HIV infection OR mother HIV-positive
classifications.treatment: routine care incl. HIV testing; infant ARV prophylaxis if mother positive
classifications.note_2014_check: 2014 generic HIV branch is coarser (CONFIRMED / HIV EXPOSED only); this finer tier is 2022-SA.

classifications.label: hiv_infection_unlikely
triggers: negative HIV test, all breastfeeding stopped >=6 weeks before test, no features
classifications.treatment: routine care; retest if indicated
classifications.note_2014_check: 2014 generic HIV branch is coarser (CONFIRMED / HIV EXPOSED only); this finer tier is 2022-SA.

features_of_hiv: persistent diarrhoea now or in past 3 months

young_infant_0_2m._note: The 0-2 month algorithm is a STRUCTURALLY DIFFERENT chart. adtc-sft-corpus-built records that the current pipeline treats young-infant prompts as a scope refusal. Transcribed here for completeness; promoting any of it out of 'refusal' is a scope decision + clinician sign-off, not just a parse.

classifications.label: yi_very_severe_disease
triggers: fast breathing >60/min or severe chest indrawing/nasal flaring/grunting
fever >=37.5C or hot / temp <35.5C or cold
umbilical redness extending to skin or draining pus
classifications.treatment: diazepam if convulsing; oxygen; ceftriaxone IM; refer urgently

classifications.label: yi_local_bacterial_infection
triggers: purulent/sticky eye discharge OR red umbilicus OR skin pustules
classifications.treatment: cephalexin/flucloxacillin; chloramphenicol eye ointment; teach home care; follow-up 2 days

classifications.label: yi_no_bacterial_infection
classifications.treatment: counsel on home care

classifications.label: yi_severe_jaundice
triggers: any jaundice if age <24 hours OR yellow palms and soles
classifications.treatment: low-blood-sugar test; keep warm; refer urgently

classifications.label: yi_jaundice
triggers: jaundice appearing after 24 hours AND palms/soles not yellow
classifications.treatment: advise return if palms/soles yellow; follow-up 1 day; refer for assessment if >14 days old

classifications.label: yi_no_jaundice
classifications.treatment: counsel on home care

classifications.label: yi_severe_dehydration
triggers: two of: lethargic/unconscious, sunken eyes, skin pinch very slow
OR young infant <1 month of age
classifications.treatment: Plan C IV; ceftriaxone IM; refer urgently

classifications.label: yi_some_dehydration
triggers: two of: restless/irritable, sunken eyes, skin pinch slow
classifications.treatment: Plan B; zinc 14 days; follow-up 2 days

classifications.label: yi_no_dehydration
triggers: not enough signs for some/severe dehydration
classifications.treatment: Plan A; zinc 14 days; follow-up 2 days

classifications.label: yi_severe_persistent_diarrhoea
classifications.treatment: treat dehydration if present; refer urgently

classifications.label: yi_dysentery
classifications.treatment: refer urgently; keep warm

congenital_problems.note: 'Was the young infant examined by a health worker after birth? If no, assess for congenital problems.' Priority signs -> urgent referral; other abnormal signs -> refer for assessment; mother RPR-positive/untreated -> congenital syphilis path.

classifications.label: yi_congenital_priority
classifications.treatment: pre-referral treatment; refer urgently
classifications.note_2014_check: young-infant congenital-problems branch not found in the 2014 generic; 2022-SA addition.

classifications.label: yi_congenital_abnormal_signs
triggers: one or more abnormal head/neck/limb/trunk signs (microcephaly, abnormal fontanelle/sutures, club foot, etc.)
classifications.treatment: keep warm; assess breastfeeding; refer for assessment
classifications.note_2014_check: young-infant congenital-problems branch not found in the 2014 generic; 2022-SA addition.

classifications.label: yi_possible_congenital_syphilis
triggers: mother RPR positive AND untreated / partially treated / treated <1 month before delivery
classifications.treatment: check for congenital syphilis signs; IM penicillin if none; refer if present

hiv_feeding_growth_immunisation.status: NOT fully transcribed -- counselling/status-check tables (HIV testing schedule, breastfed/non-breastfed feeding & growth, immunisation status). Page refs recorded; transcribe on demand if promoted out of the young-infant refusal.

not_transcribed.note: Pages that are treatment-dosing, counselling, ART protocol, skin problems, growth charts, or follow-up tables rather than assess/classify decision tables. Listed so nothing is silently dropped.
